label: Accretion Ledger
design_principles: Impossibility beats incentives. Accept chaos, calcify permanence. Any amount, any time, no key until maturity. Remove willpower from the equation.
core_mechanism: Time-locked cryptographic commitment: contributions are mathematically inaccessible until retirement date. Not penalized—the unlock keys don't exist until then.
how_it_works: Workers contribute any amount via app to smart contract. Contribution buys time-locked tokens with unlock date set to their 65th birthday. Transaction costs near-zero via L2. At retirement, contract auto-releases monthly proportional amounts. Community Pool option: workers join shared contract with proportional claim tracking. Research needed: oracle reliability, cross-chain time-lock standards, regulatory classification.
what_is_new: Replaces withdrawal penalties with withdrawal impossibility. No minimums, no schedules, no willpower required. Accepts income chaos as input, outputs guaranteed permanence.
why_it_works: Irregular earners fail traditional systems because every drought tempts them to raid savings. Penalties don't stop desperate people. Mathematical impossibility does. The harshness IS the feature—it's the only thing that survives contact with real poverty.
why_it_fails: Genuine emergencies exist—medical crises, family disasters. A system with zero flexibility condemns some people to preventable catastrophe while their locked wealth sits unusable. The cruelty of forced patience is real.
medium_term: Parallel system emerges for gig workers: 15% of irregular earners using accretion ledgers show higher retirement security than those with traditional 401k access. Behavioral economists study the 'impossibility advantage.'
long_term_vision: Retirement security decoupled from income regularity. Gig workers, artists, seasonal laborers accumulate futures through micro-contributions that survive their own desperation. The question 'how much do you earn monthly?' becomes irrelevant to retirement planning.

label: Guaranteed Capacity Mutual
design_principles: Skills are the primary retirement asset, not money. Income droughts are forced investment periods. Future labor becomes liquid through collective guarantee of delivery.
core_mechanism: Pooled labor futures with collective delivery guarantee: members sell tradeable tokens for future work hours, backed by statistical certainty across the mutual.
how_it_works: Variable earners join a Capacity Mutual. During high-income: contribute cash AND log verified skills. During low-income: receive funded retraining (not cash) plus can sell 'labor future tokens' for immediate liquidity. Tokens represent hours of future work in your skill areas. The mutual guarantees delivery—if you can't fulfill, another qualified member does. Retirement: your accumulated tokens are sellable assets, OR you fulfill others' tokens at reduced hours, OR you teach/mentor (scaling your capacity without physical labor). Research needed: actuarial models for labor fulfillment rates, skill verification systems, token pricing mechanisms.
what_is_new: Inverts the crisis model: income droughts trigger skill investment, not emergency withdrawals. Creates liquid market for illiquid human capital through collective guarantee. Retirement savings denominated in verified capacity, not just currency.
why_it_works: Variable earners fail traditional systems because droughts drain savings. This system converts droughts into compulsory skill accumulation—each crisis leaves you more employable. The collective guarantee solves the core problem of human capital illiquidity: your future labor becomes tradeable TODAY because 500 people backstop delivery.
why_it_fails: Requires critical mass to function—small mutuals can't guarantee delivery. Skill verification is expensive and gameable. Members may resent 'forced training' during crises when they need cash. If the mutual's skill mix doesn't match market demand, tokens become worthless. Adverse selection: the most reliable workers have less need to join.
medium_term: Pilot mutuals form around stable skill clusters (licensed trades, healthcare aides). Labor future tokens begin trading on specialized exchanges. Employment gaps become 'capacity sabbaticals' on resumes.
long_term_vision: Human capital becomes as liquid as financial capital. Variable earners accumulate tradeable skill portfolios rather than anxiously hoarding cash. 'Retirement' means shifting from fulfilling tokens to teaching others to fulfill them—a gradual transition, not a cliff. The question changes from 'do you have enough saved?' to 'what can you verifiably do?'

label: Impression Pool
design_principles: You cannot save a balance, only leave marks. Enforced ignorance prevents obsessive optimization. Dispersed patterns outperform concentrated ones.
core_mechanism: Contributions convert to timestamped pattern-parameters in a collective pool. No individual balance exists—only your impression history, which shapes claims at retirement.
how_it_works: Join pool, get ID. Contribute any amount anytime—system logs timestamp, size-tier (1-5), and pool conditions. Money enters collective investment immediately. No balance to check. At retirement, algorithm processes your impressions: more impressions = higher base; time-dispersed = multiplier; contributions during downturns = loyalty bonus. Monthly payout = f(your_pattern, pool_value, all_patterns). Research needed: algorithm transparency mechanisms, independent auditing, pattern-weighting fairness studies.
what_is_new: Eliminates the 'balance' entirely. You cannot track progress because there's nothing discrete to track—only contribution events. Variable income creates dispersed patterns that may outperform consistent savers. Withdrawal is meaningless (erasing patterns doesn't return money).
why_it_works: Variable earners fail systems requiring consistent tracking and optimization. This system rewards showing up irregularly over time, not hitting targets. You can't obsess over what doesn't exist. True pooled risk means your bad years are absorbed by others' good years. The ignorance isn't cruel—it's protective.
why_it_fails: Radical opacity breeds distrust. People want to know if they're 'on track.' The algorithm is a black box—who sets the weights? No portability: if the pool fails, your patterns are worthless. Early death means your impressions benefit others but not your heirs. Some will refuse to participate in anything they can't monitor.
medium_term: Behavioral research shows impression-pool participants report lower financial anxiety despite (because of?) ignorance. Gig platforms integrate automatic micro-contributions. Algorithm auditing becomes a regulated profession.
long_term_vision: Retirement security measured by 'impression density' rather than net worth. Variable earners recognized as naturally suited to pattern-based retirement—their irregularity becomes advantage. The question 'how much have you saved?' becomes unanswerable and irrelevant, replaced by 'how long have you been contributing?' Financial anxiety shifts from tracking to simply participating.

label: Residue Rights Pension
design_principles: You contribute by working, not by saving. Your digital exhaust is your retirement asset. Hour-denomination defeats inflation. Capture what already exists.
core_mechanism: Mandatory worker ownership of work-generated data, automatically harvested and monetized, with credits denominated in median-wage-hours to ensure inflation-proof value.
how_it_works: Legislation establishes workers own data their work activity generates. A national trust captures this through employer systems, platform APIs, and voluntary device connections. Data is anonymized and sold (AI training, logistics optimization, market research). Revenue converts to 'median-hour credits'—denominated in hours of current median wage, not currency. At retirement, credits redeem as income: 50,000 credits × current median hourly wage. Workers never consciously contribute—their activity exhaust is captured automatically. Research needed: data valuation models, privacy-preserving aggregation, cross-platform attribution.
what_is_new: Zero-effort contribution. Your work generates retirement assets whether you save or not. Variable income becomes completely irrelevant—a gig worker doing 2000 hours of delivery generates more credits than a consultant doing 1000 hours of meetings. Decouples retirement from earnings entirely.
why_it_works: Variable earners fail traditional systems because income droughts prevent saving. But they're ALWAYS generating data value—every delivery, every shift, every task leaves traces. Platforms already capture billions in surveillance value from workers who save nothing. Redirecting this exhaust to workers creates retirement funding from activity that already happens. The more you work, the more you accumulate—regardless of what you're paid.
why_it_fails: Surveillance concerns are massive—even with anonymization, workers may resist mandatory data capture. Data value could collapse as AI saturates on training data. Employers might minimize worker data generation to reduce costs. Attribution across platforms is technically hard. Gig economy giants will lobby viciously against losing their data advantage.
medium_term: Gig platforms required to report worker-data-value alongside wages. 'Data contribution statements' become standard. Variable earners discover their retirement growing despite saving nothing. New profession emerges: 'exhaust auditors' who verify attribution.
long_term_vision: Retirement security determined by lifetime work-hours, not lifetime savings. Variable earners, gig workers, and caregivers accumulate substantial retirement through activity that already happens. The question shifts from 'how much did you save?' to 'how much did you do?' Platform surveillance capitalism partially redirected toward worker benefit. Those who work hardest—regardless of pay—retire most securely.

label: Obligation Web Pension
design_principles: Work creates debt. Those who benefit from labor owe the laborer. Relationship diversity beats savings depth. The worker contributes nothing—their work already did.
core_mechanism: Every verified work relationship automatically generates retirement obligation credits against benefiting entities—legal liens that accumulate without worker action and convert to retirement income when called.
how_it_works: Work relationships logged via platform APIs, employer systems, gig apps to immutable ledger. Each relationship generates 'obligation units' (duration × verified intensity) as legal claims against that entity. At retirement, entities pay into your fund proportional to accumulated units—or provide equivalent services. Diverse work history = diverse creditor portfolio = resilience. Entity bankruptcy risk pooled across industry-level insurance. Research needed: legal framework for mandatory work-liens, cross-platform attribution standards, obligation unit valuation.
what_is_new: Inverts retirement funding: beneficiaries of labor pay, not laborers. Irregular earners with many gigs accumulate obligation units faster than steady workers with one employer. Your retirement portfolio is entities that owe you, not money you saved. Fragmented work history becomes diversified creditor base.
why_it_works: Variable earners can't save money consistently but DO work consistently—just across many relationships. By making work itself create automatic claims, we convert the one reliable thing they do into retirement security. Gig economy fragmentation transforms from retirement threat to advantage: more platforms touched = more entities obligated = more resilient retirement.
why_it_fails: Employers will minimize documented work relationships to reduce liability. Small entities may fail before obligations mature. Political resistance to mandatory work-liens will be fierce. Creates incentive for under-the-table work. Verification across informal work is technically hard. Early career workers subsidize entities they'll never collect from.
medium_term: Gig platforms required to report obligation accruals alongside earnings. 'Obligation portfolio diversity score' becomes standard metric. Workers actively seek varied work relationships for retirement security. New profession emerges: obligation auditors who verify cross-platform work histories.
long_term_vision: Retirement security determined by breadth of work relationships, not savings rate. Variable earners with diverse histories retire more securely than single-employer workers. The question shifts from 'how much did you save?' to 'how many entities owe you?' Gig economy's fragmentation—once a crisis—becomes its workers' greatest retirement advantage. Those who worked everywhere retire best.

label: Tidal Cohort Mutual
design_principles: Variable income is system advantage, not deficit. Low-earning periods produce liquidity commitment + service labor. Collective thresholds over individual balances. Older contributions genuinely harder to access, not just penalized.
core_mechanism: Dual-currency retirement cohorts: Deposit (money) + Presence (non-withdrawal commitment and service labor), where both count toward collective benefit thresholds and variable earners are valued for counter-cyclical system contribution.
how_it_works: Join cohort of 12-30 people with varied income patterns. High-earning: deposit money (creates rebalancing load). Low-earning: earn Presence credits via binding non-withdrawal commitment + mandatory service hours (verification, governance, mentorship). Benefits unlock when COHORT hits collective thresholds, incentivizing mutual aid. Contributions auto-migrate to increasingly illiquid investments with age. Heavy contributors gain governance rights AND binding obligations. Research needed: optimal cohort composition algorithms, Presence-to-Deposit conversion ratios, liquidity graduation schedules, service hour verification systems.
what_is_new: First system to treat low-earning periods as productive contribution rather than absence. Variable earners provide genuine system value (timing diversity, liquidity commitment, service capacity) that steady earners cannot. Earnings irregularity becomes competitive advantage.
why_it_works: Traditional retirement penalizes irregular income because it measures only deposit. But pools NEED what low-deposit periods provide: someone available to work the pool, commitment to not withdraw, counter-cyclical timing. By measuring both currencies, variable earners finally receive credit for the system services their income pattern naturally provides.
why_it_fails: Presence valuation is gameable - members may minimize real service while claiming hours. Collective thresholds create free-rider problems within cohorts. Cohort formation may stratify by income class despite intentions. Service labor is tedious; members may resent mandatory governance. Illiquid investments genuinely trap capital when life circumstances change.
medium_term: Gig platforms offer cohort-matching features. 'Income pattern diversity score' becomes positive metric for cohort health. Service hours develop market value as transferable credentials. Variable earners report higher retirement confidence than steady earners in traditional plans.
long_term_vision: Retirement security measured by cohort health rather than individual balance. Variable earners recognized as essential infrastructure of mutual pools - their irregularity providing the timing diversity, service capacity, and liquidity commitment that make collective retirement function. The question shifts from 'how much have you saved?' to 'what cohort do you belong to, and what role do you play in it?'

label: Drift Yield System
design_principles: Anxiety is the enemy, not variance. Reward demonstrated acceptance. Make optimization impossible by design. Income chaos becomes structural advantage.
core_mechanism: Stochastic contribution timing + variance-based return multipliers + costly balance checks: the less you predict, control, or monitor, the better your returns.
how_it_works: Enroll with annual contribution ceiling. System randomly micro-drafts throughout year via opaque algorithm—you know roughly how much annually, never when. Balance checks cost 0.5% + reset neglect timer. Quarterly 'neglect bonuses' compound for non-checkers. Income variance score (from contribution pattern volatility) determines return multiplier: high variance = 1.15x, medium = 1.0x, low = 0.95x. Withdrawal requires 24-month irrevocable notice. Research needed: optimal variance thresholds, draft algorithm parameters, behavioral effects of check-pricing.
what_is_new: First system to structurally reward income volatility rather than tolerate it. Unpredictable contribution timing makes anxiety impossible rather than inadvisable. Explicitly prices attention as a cost and neglect as an asset.
why_it_works: Documented: investors who check frequently underperform by 1.5% annually from panic selling and over-trading. Variable earners already practice financial acceptance daily—they can't form rigid expectations. This system rewards the psychology they already have and penalizes the anxious optimization that destroys steady earners' wealth.
why_it_fails: Some genuinely need to check—medical emergencies, job loss verification. Pricing attention feels paternalistic and may trigger resentment. Variance rewards could be gamed by artificially varying income patterns. The 24-month notice period is cruel for actual emergencies. May attract only those already prone to neglect, creating adverse selection.
medium_term: Gig platforms offer 'Drift Yield' integration. Income volatility scores become positive metrics on retirement dashboards. Behavioral research confirms: Drift participants check 1/20th as often, report 40% lower financial anxiety, outperform traditional 401k by 2% annually.
long_term_vision: Retirement anxiety decoupled from income pattern. Variable earners—once the most vulnerable—become the most secure because their income chaos trained them for the only retirement approach that actually works: acceptance. The question shifts from 'how consistent is your income?' to 'how comfortable are you with not knowing?' Financial health measured by ability to forget, not ability to track.

label: Presence Pool
design_principles: Sustained relationship beats accumulated deposits. The pool recognizes those who stayed through good times and bad. Contribution amounts don't determine benefits—presence does.
core_mechanism: Withdrawal rights based on tenure-years × presence-density, independent of contribution magnitude. Auto-contribute when able; maintain presence always; receive based on how long you've belonged.
how_it_works: Join trade/neighborhood pool. Auto-sweep contributions when income detected (open banking)—any amount, no individual tracking. Monthly 'presence witness': brief app engagement viewing pool health. No individual balance shown—only tenure-density score. At retirement: your payout share = your (tenure × presence) / total pool (tenure × presence). Research needed: minimum viable pool size, presence verification against automation, portability standards.
what_is_new: Withdrawal rights decoupled from contribution amounts. Identical tenure-presence = identical payout regardless of contribution history. Presence during zero-income periods counts equally to presence during prosperity.
why_it_works: Variable earners can't reliably save but CAN reliably maintain presence—a monthly check-in costs nothing. By measuring tenure and presence instead of deposits, the system asks for something variable earners can actually provide: sustained membership. The redistribution is feature, not bug—social insurance through collective membership.
why_it_fails: High earners may refuse if contributions don't benefit them proportionally—though tax incentives and community ties help. Presence verification gameable via automation. Pools may be underfunded if amounts don't matter. Political resistance to explicit redistribution is severe.
medium_term: Gig platforms integrate presence pools. 'Tenure years' becomes portable retirement metric. Variable earners report lower anxiety as contribution pressure dissolves into presence maintenance.
long_term_vision: Retirement measured by belonging duration, not savings accumulation. Variable earners thrive because their only obligation—sustained presence—is achievable in any income state. The question shifts from 'how much have you saved?' to 'how long have you belonged?' Retirement becomes community membership rather than individual accumulation.

label: Forbearance-Credited Retirement Pool
design_principles: Hardship forbearance is measurable contribution. Your worst months are your best deposits. The pool explicitly pays for the value your non-withdrawal creates.
core_mechanism: Dual-path contribution: earning periods deposit money, non-earning periods earn Forbearance Credit Units proportional to the verified pool value your non-withdrawal creates.
how_it_works: Join pool with income-linked monitoring (open banking). Earning: auto-contribute percentage. Non-earning (income below threshold): earn FCUs = (potential withdrawal) × (pool return that month) × (hardship multiplier). Hardship multiplier increases with verified income severity. Counter-cyclical twins matched algorithmically—their contributions during your drought stabilize pool while your forbearance earns credits. Monthly FCU value published transparently: 'Your non-withdrawal saved the pool $X this month.' At retirement: monetary contributions + FCU value combined. Research needed: optimal hardship multiplier curves, twin-matching algorithms, forbearance verification standards.
what_is_new: First system to explicitly quantify and credit the economic value of NOT withdrawing. Income gaps become premium contribution periods because forbearance during hardship prevents costly forced liquidations. The poorer your month, the richer your contribution.
why_it_works: Pools lose 15-40% during panics from forced sales at depressed prices. Members who don't withdraw prevent this loss—but currently receive zero credit. By attributing this saved value to forbearers, irregular earners accumulate substantial retirement during their hardest periods. Their volatility becomes structural advantage: more forbearance opportunities = more FCU accumulation.
why_it_fails: People must survive during forbearance periods—if survival requires depleting other assets or taking predatory loans, the system just shifts the burden. Verification of genuine hardship versus gaming is administratively complex. Pools may resist explicit value attribution because it reduces collective surplus.
medium_term: Forbearance Credit statements become standard: 'Your non-withdrawal contributed $2,400 in pool stability this quarter.' Irregular earners report higher retirement security than steady earners in traditional plans. Financial planning inverts: 'How do I maximize my forbearance credits during the next downturn?'
long_term_vision: Retirement security decoupled from earning consistency. Income droughts—once catastrophic—become premium contribution periods. The question shifts from 'how much did you save?' to 'how much value did your forbearance create?' Irregular earners recognized as pool stabilizers whose volatility provides the forbearance-capacity that steady earners cannot. Those who struggled most retire strongest.

label: Testimony Accord
design_principles: Sincerity outweighs amount. Witnessed struggle creates obligation. Public commitment is binding. The quality of your expressed sacrifice determines your claim.
core_mechanism: Contributions weighted by testified sacrifice-level: video-recorded commitment ceremonies scored for sincerity and hardship-context multiply retirement claims beyond raw amounts.
how_it_works: Join pool. When contributing: record 60-second testimony explaining what you're sacrificing and why. AI + peer witnesses score sincerity (0.5-3x multiplier). Hardship-context detected via income verification adds additional multiplier (up to 5x for contributions during crisis). Zero-income periods: record hardship testimony that earns 'struggle credits' even without money. Accumulated testimony-weight (not just dollars) determines proportional claim on collective pool. Research needed: sincerity-detection algorithms, gaming-resistant verification, cross-cultural calibration.
what_is_new: First system where HOW you contribute matters more than HOW MUCH. A sincere $50 during hardship can outweigh a casual $500 during abundance. Variable earners with authentic struggle narratives accumulate faster than comfortable steady earners.
why_it_works: Variable earners have the most sincere sacrifice stories—every contribution is genuinely hard-won. Traditional systems ignore this; we monetize it. Public testimony creates psychological commitment (behavioral economics), social obligation (community witnessed your promise), and emotional investment that makes the pool feel like mutual aid rather than faceless institution.
why_it_fails: Sincerity-scoring is gameable—the articulate may outperform the genuine. AI bias in testimony assessment could encode cultural prejudices. Some find public testimony humiliating. Peer-witnessing at scale is administratively heavy. The system may inadvertently reward performance of poverty rather than actual struggle.
medium_term: Testimony libraries emerge as collective memory. Employers integrate contribution ceremonies into workplace culture. 'Testimony wealth' becomes recognized metric alongside financial wealth. Irregular earners report higher retirement security and pool belonging than steady earners in traditional plans.
long_term_vision: Retirement security measured by accumulated expressed commitment rather than accumulated savings. Variable earners—whose every contribution requires genuine sacrifice—become the wealthiest in testimony-weight. The question shifts from 'how much did you save?' to 'how sincerely did you commit?' Financial planning becomes a practice of authentic public reckoning with one's struggles and hopes, building pools that feel like communities of witnessed mutual promise rather than anonymous asset accumulation.

label: Downstream Positioning Pool
design_principles: Retirement is relocation, not accumulation. Position to catch overflow, not extract contributions. Engineer counter-cyclical diversity. True dormancy during scarcity.
core_mechanism: Dynamic sufficiency-floor overflow capture feeding counter-cyclically diverse pools that invest in building low-cost exit communities where members relocate upon three-factor alignment.
how_it_works: Open banking calculates personal sufficiency floor from spending patterns. Only amounts exceeding floor by threshold auto-sweep to pool—during scarcity, complete dormancy (no credits, presence, or tracking). Pools recruit for income-cycle diversity via pattern analysis. Pool invests in/partners with purpose-built retirement communities in lower-cost areas. Retirement triggers when personal share, pool liquidity, and market conditions align—not at fixed age. Retiring means relocating downstream where your share buys 2-3x the life. Research needed: sufficiency-floor algorithms, cycle-diversity optimization, exit-community economic modeling.
what_is_new: First system where retirement is primarily geographic repositioning rather than wealth accumulation. First to require true dormancy during scarcity (no credits, presence, or interaction—not even forbearance tracking). First to engineer counter-cyclical pool composition through selective recruitment rather than hoping for diversification.
why_it_works: Variable earners fail systems requiring consistent action during droughts. This asks nothing during scarcity—complete dormancy. The exit-community model means the accumulation target is 40-60% of traditional thresholds. Counter-cyclical recruitment means individual volatility smooths at pool level. You don't need to save 'enough for retirement'—you need enough for downstream admission, which is achievable.
why_it_fails: Exit communities require massive upfront investment and may become isolated enclaves. Selective recruitment could exclude those with 'unfavorable' income patterns. Three-factor alignment may strand people in extended waiting. Geographic relocation severs social ties. Sufficiency-floor gaming possible. Those who can't/won't relocate receive no benefit from the core mechanism.
medium_term: Pilot exit communities emerge in lower-cost regions with deliberate economic design. 'Downstream admission threshold' becomes a retirement planning metric alongside traditional targets. Cycle-diversity scores appear on pool dashboards.
long_term_vision: Retirement reframed as earned access to abundance-positioned communities rather than individual wealth accumulation. Variable earners, whose income chaos created the best overflow patterns, retire earlier than steady earners by relocating downstream. The question shifts from 'have you saved enough?' to 'have you earned downstream admission?' Geographic arbitrage, once a desperate individual choice, becomes structured collective infrastructure.

label: Defense-Weighted Claim Accumulation
design_principles: Judgment quality outweighs deposit quantity. Peer scrutiny crystallizes claim value. Reasoning under pressure is the scarce resource variable earners already possess—monetize it.
core_mechanism: Contributions earn multiplied claim value (0.5x-3x) based on peer-evaluated quality of the financial reasoning behind them, not the amount deposited.
how_it_works: Variable earners join circles of 8-15 members with diverse income patterns. Any contribution (or defended zero-contribution) enters a defense queue with brief rationale. Rotating peer panels challenge weak reasoning, approve strong reasoning, assign multipliers. $50 with excellent reasoning (clear trade-off analysis, alternatives considered, circumstances documented) earns more claim value than $200 with lazy reasoning. Zero-contribution months defended well ('boat repairs required full surplus—here's documentation and recovery plan') maintain standing without penalty. Claims compound in pooled investment. Transition to 'Senior Evaluator' after threshold reached: paid to review others' defenses, train new members, assess financial products. Research needed: peer review incentive design, multiplier calibration, defense quality rubrics.
what_is_new: First system where HOW you reasoned about contributing matters more than HOW MUCH you contributed. Transforms variable earners' constant financial recalculation from burden into retirement-building activity. Defense of non-contribution is as valid as defense of contribution.
why_it_works: Variable earners already possess the scarce skill this system values—the capacity to make sound financial judgments under uncertainty. Traditional systems ignore this, asking only for consistent deposits they can't provide. This system rewards the judgment they exercise constantly. Peer scrutiny creates accountability without requiring consistency. The multiplier mechanism means irregular earners with excellent reasoning accumulate faster than steady earners who never examine their choices.
why_it_fails: Articulate people may game the system with convincing defenses of poor choices—the 'eloquence problem.' Peer review is time-intensive and could feel burdensome. Cultural variations in argumentation style may disadvantage some groups. Some will find public defense of financial circumstances humiliating. Multiplier calibration is complex—too generous rewards gaming, too stingy defeats the purpose.
medium_term: Financial reasoning scores become portable credentials. Gig platforms integrate defense-based contribution options. 'Judgment quality index' appears alongside credit scores. Variable earners report higher retirement confidence and financial self-awareness than steady earners in traditional plans.
long_term_vision: Retirement security measured by accumulated demonstrated judgment rather than accumulated deposits. Variable earners—forced to constantly reason about irregular circumstances—become the most skilled retirement accumulators. The question shifts from 'how much did you save?' to 'how well did you think about what you could save?' Financial wisdom, not just financial amounts, becomes inheritable through the evaluation traditions passed to new members.

label: Ripened Departure Mutual
design_principles: Retirement security from exit quality, not entry amount. Variable earners excel at preparing clean transfers. Position earned through service, claimed through departure execution.
core_mechanism: Intergenerational pools where retirement claims depend on documented skill/knowledge transfer quality (ripened departures), not contribution amounts.
how_it_works: Join 20-person pool spanning generations. Register 'departure commitments'—skills you'll transfer at retirement. Document over years (ripening). Low-income: earn credits via pool service. At retirement: structured 'mass departure' ceremonies transfer commitments to successors. Receivers rate quality; ratings determine claim level. Elders receive from ongoing provider departures. Research: transfer metrics, pool composition, ripening incentives.
what_is_new: First system measuring retirement by EXIT quality, not entry amount. Asks what variable earners CAN give (transferable documentation, service) not what they can't (consistent deposits).
why_it_works: Variable earners build diverse skills across many contexts—natural 'departure artists.' Traditional systems measure the wrong thing. Transfer capacity accumulates regardless of income. Documentation and service happen in any income state.
why_it_fails: Transfer quality is subjective and gameable. Documentation feels like unpaid labor. Small pools risk adverse selection. Physical laborers with non-transferable skills disadvantaged. Receivers may score unfairly. 'Mass departure' ceremony may feel performative.
medium_term: 'Departure documentation' services emerge. 'Transfer readiness scores' appear on credentials. Informal expertise becomes documented, tradeable retirement asset.
long_term_vision: Retirement reframed from 'saved enough' to 'prepared transfers well enough.' Variable earners—with diverse, transferable skills—retire more securely than single-career workers with savings but nothing to give. Question: 'how much have you saved?' → 'what can you cleanly leave behind?'

label: Crystallization Guild
design_principles: Variability IS stability when patterns diversify. Collective governance creates real economic value. Annual crystallization erases month-to-month chaos. Build only on what's true.
core_mechanism: Pattern-diverse guilds with invisible intra-year pooling, annual crystallization, and governance participation that directly multiplies returns—making engagement economically equivalent to contribution.
how_it_works: Join guild of 20-50 members recruited for income pattern diversity (seasonal, gig, commission, creative). Contributions auto-sweep but interface shows nothing until year-end—not 'checking costs money' but 'there's nothing to check.' Monthly meetings: collective investment votes, strategy decisions. Year-end crystallization reveals annual total, converts to locked credits. Returns allocated: (crystallized credits) × (governance participation score). Research needed: diversity metrics, governance-return attribution, participation verification.
what_is_new: First to explicitly value income pattern diversity as collective asset. First with genuine contribution invisibility (no tracking interface, not penalized tracking). First where governance participation has direct return multiplication—converting available regularity into economic value.
why_it_works: Variable earners can't provide contribution regularity but CAN provide pattern diversity and governance engagement. By making these economically equivalent to deposits, we convert their actual assets into retirement. The invisibility removes month-to-month shame; the diversity framing transforms stigma into value; governance gives economic weight to the regularity they can actually provide.
why_it_fails: Democratic investment decisions have mixed performance records—collective governance may underperform professional management. Pattern diversity is complex to measure and verify. Participation scoring invites gaming. Those unable to attend meetings are disadvantaged regardless of contributions. Self-sorting may undermine diversity goals.
medium_term: 'Pattern diversity score' becomes recognized guild health metric. Governance track records create reputational capital. Variable earners report transformed self-perception: from 'inconsistent saver' to 'diversifying contributor.' Annual crystallization becomes a ritual with meaning.
long_term_vision: Retirement security rests on three equal pillars: financial contribution, pattern diversity, and governance skill. Variable earners recognized as essential infrastructure—their unpredictability creates collective stability through diversification. The question shifts from 'how consistent is your income?' to 'what pattern do you bring, and how well do you govern?' Income variability reframed from personal failure to structural asset. Guilds compete on diversity and governance quality, not just returns.

label: Maturation Queue Retirement
design_principles: Irregularity is architecture, not deficit. Each deposit is its own clock. No balance exists—only maturation queues. Withdrawal isn't penalized; it's architecturally absent.
core_mechanism: Every contribution becomes a separate locked cell with individual maturity date (max of age 62, deposit+25yrs). Cells mature FIFO, each releasing over 36 months. No unified balance—only queue depth.
how_it_works: Deposit any amount anytime via app to smart contract factory. Each creates independent contract instance with NO withdrawal function (not penalty—function absent). Maturity = max(your_age_62, deposit_date+25yrs). Upon maturity, contract auto-distributes over 36 months. You see: 'X cells, next maturation in Y months, estimated monthly release Z.' Gaps in deposits create gaps in future income—but bursts create bursts. Research needed: cross-platform contract standards, survivor benefit mechanisms, inflation indexing per cell.
what_is_new: First system where deposits maintain discrete identity forever. No balance aggregation, no pattern-scoring, no unified tracking. Your retirement income SHAPE replicates your working-life contribution SHAPE with 25-40 year delay. Irregularity isn't smoothed—it's faithfully transmitted.
why_it_works: Variable earners fail systems requiring consistency or balance-watching. This system has no balance to watch and no consistency to maintain. Each deposit is complete in itself. Gaps aren't failures—they're gaps in a queue, creating later gaps in retirement (which may align with lower-need periods). The time-delayed echo means working-life rhythms repeat in retirement, which may feel natural rather than jarring.
why_it_fails: Retirement income volatility may be worse than working-life volatility (less adaptive capacity at 70). Extreme earners may have years with no maturing cells. No smoothing mechanism means no protection against 'all feast no famine' workers who need consistent income. Long gap periods create retirement deserts. Early deposits in youth may mature before actual retirement need.
medium_term: Gig platforms integrate 'cell deposit' buttons. Workers develop intuition for 'queue shape'—not balance, but maturation schedule. 'Cells per decade' becomes retirement metric. Variable earners report lower anxiety from balance-absence.
long_term_vision: Retirement decoupled from consistency. Workers accumulate maturation queues rather than balances, receiving time-shifted echoes of working lives. The question shifts from 'how much have you saved?' to 'what does your maturation queue look like?' Income irregularity stops being retirement disqualification—it's just a queue shape, faithfully preserved and delivered decades later.

label: Arc-Synced Trajectory Retirement
design_principles: Measure adequacy against your predicted arc, not universal standards. No balance—only trajectory completion. The goalposts move with your reality. Variable income is the native case, not the exception.
core_mechanism: ML-predicted lifetime earning curves set personalized contribution trajectories. Auto-contributions sync to earning rhythm. Retirement readiness = trajectory completion percentage, not accumulated balance.
how_it_works: Enroll: ML models predict your lifetime earning arc from profession, patterns, demographics. System calculates target trajectory (% of predicted earnings). Open banking detects income, auto-sweeps proportional contributions. Dashboard shows only trajectory completion (never balance). High-earning periods contribute more; droughts contribute nothing without penalty—you're measured against YOUR arc. Yearly recalculation adjusts arc to reality: career change, disability, or windfall shifts the target, so you can't fall behind a moving goalpost. Pooled investment; claims = trajectory completion × arc-length. Research needed: trajectory model accuracy by profession, bias auditing, recalculation frequency optimization.
what_is_new: First system where retirement adequacy is measured against personalized earning predictions rather than universal targets. No balance display—only trajectory completion %. Goals automatically adjust to life changes, making 'falling behind' structurally impossible.
why_it_works: Variable earners fail systems measuring them against steady-income assumptions. This system measures them against THEIR predicted trajectory. A gig worker earning sporadically but contributing proportionally shows 100% trajectory completion—identical to salaried workers. The moving goalpost eliminates the psychological trap of 'behind-ness' that causes people to abandon retirement planning entirely.
why_it_fails: ML trajectory predictions may be systematically wrong, giving false confidence. Low-earning predictions could lock disadvantaged workers into low retirement expectations (algorithmic ceiling). Trajectory completion % may feel too abstract—people want to see dollars. Those who outperform predictions may resent that their 'above-arc' contributions don't proportionally increase claims.
medium_term: 'Trajectory completion' becomes standard metric alongside credit scores. Gig platforms integrate arc-synced contribution. Workers report lower retirement anxiety as the question shifts from 'am I saving enough?' to 'am I on my arc?' Actuarial profession expands into personal trajectory modeling.
long_term_vision: Retirement readiness decoupled from income regularity and absolute amounts. Variable earners achieve identical trajectory completion to steady earners by contributing proportionally during earning phases. The question 'how much have you saved?' becomes meaningless—replaced by 'how well does your contribution pattern match your earning pattern?' Financial anxiety shifts from accumulation to alignment. Those whose lives change drastically aren't punished—their arcs simply recalculate, keeping them perpetually 'on track' relative to their actual circumstances.

label: Engagement Resonance Retirement
design_principles: Engagement frequency > contribution magnitude. Hide amounts, show bonds. Variable income = more engagement opportunities = faster resonance building. Relationship with system matters, not deposits to it.
core_mechanism: Income-triggered binary engagements accumulate 'resonance scores'; claims based on engagement history, not contribution history; amounts deliberately hidden from participant.
how_it_works: Open banking detects income events. Each event allows one 'engagement' (auto-contribution of fixed %, amount hidden). System records timestamp + cryptographic proof. Resonance score = f(engagement count, time-spread, pattern irregularity). Pool invests collectively. Retirement: your_share = your_resonance/total_resonance × pool_value. Need-gradient amplifies payout efficiency for those with lower external assets. Research needed: resonance algorithms, income-verification standards, optimal percentage.
what_is_new: First system where claim strength is based on contribution FREQUENCY rather than contribution AMOUNT. Variable earners with many small income events build resonance faster than steady earners with few large events. Amounts deliberately obscured—you see only engagement count and pattern metrics.
why_it_works: Variable earners have MORE income events than steady earners—every gig, sale, shift is engagement opportunity. By rewarding engagement frequency, fragmented income becomes structural advantage. Hidden amounts eliminate contribution-size anxiety. Need-gradient ensures desperate retirees draw efficiently from modest bonds.
why_it_fails: Creates redistribution toward high-frequency earners, which may feel unfair to high-amount/low-frequency earners. Sophisticated actors might fragment income artificially. Hidden amounts may breed distrust. Those with large infrequent incomes (annual contracts, quarterly bonuses) are structurally disadvantaged despite equal earnings.
medium_term: Gig platforms integrate 'engage' buttons post-payment. 'Resonance score' appears on financial profiles. Variable earners report transformed relationship with retirement—each small gig becomes engagement opportunity. Income fragmentation, once stigmatized, becomes retirement strategy.
long_term_vision: Retirement security measured by engagement density rather than savings rate. Variable earners—whose fragmented income once excluded them—become highest-resonance contributors. The question shifts from 'how much have you saved?' to 'how often have you engaged?' The anxious tracking of amounts dissolves into simple relationship maintenance: when you earn, you touch the system, and the system remembers.

label: Recognition-Flow Retirement
design_principles: Being known beats having claims. Relationship depth is the asset, not accumulated balance. Provision flows toward the recognized, not claimed by creditors.
core_mechanism: AI-mediated individual modeling where lifetime engagement creates recognition-depth that determines predictive, need-matched retirement provision flowing automatically to participants.
how_it_works: Join system, begin being modeled. Engage throughout life: deposits when possible, but also circumstance updates, relationship connections, need expressions. System builds increasingly rich model of you—patterns, context, network, needs. Variable contributions don't matter; model depth does. At retirement: no withdrawal requests. System proactively flows resources based on predictive model of your needs. Deeper model = more accurate anticipation = better-matched provision. Recognition-bonds with other members add to your depth—their knowing of you compounds with system's knowing. Research needed: privacy-preserving individual modeling, need-prediction accuracy metrics, recognition-bond activation thresholds.
what_is_new: First system where qualitative 'being known' determines provision—not contribution amounts, engagement frequency, or pattern-parameters. First where provision flows TO you based on predictive recognition rather than being claimed BY you. First where relationships are recognition-bonds that activate based on need-differential, not obligations that strain under pressure.
why_it_works: Variable earners can build recognition-depth regardless of income—the model deepens through any authentic engagement. A poor month is still a month of being known. Gaming is structurally impossible: you can't fake decades of authentic being-known, and if you try, the model captures that inauthenticity. The system provides based on actually knowing you, not based on stored claims that may not match your needs.
why_it_fails: Building and maintaining individual AI models at scale raises massive privacy concerns—who controls the model of you? 'Recognition depth' is inherently subjective and the algorithm encoding it will embed biases. Private people who engage minimally despite genuine participation will have thin models. Predictive provision could be paternalistic or wrong. The system knowing you deeply is as dystopian as it is supportive.
medium_term: Gig platforms integrate recognition-building engagement. 'Model depth' becomes a portable metric. Variable earners report transformed relationship with retirement—not anxious accumulation but ongoing connection. Privacy frameworks emerge for individual AI models as personal property.
long_term_vision: Retirement security measured by relationship quality with systems that know you, not by accumulated claims you hold. Variable earners thrive because recognition-depth grows through any authentic engagement regardless of contribution amounts. The question shifts from 'how much have you saved?' to 'how well are you known?' Provision becomes anticipatory rather than claimed—the system that knows you provides what you need before you ask. Financial anxiety dissolves into relationship maintenance: stay known, stay supported.

label: Triadic Trust Tenure
design_principles: Security from position, not possession. Elders contribute present value, not past work. Smoothing at commune level, not individual. Make isolation expensive, not illegal. Three generations need each other.
core_mechanism: Intergenerational cooperatives (7-12 spanning three life-stages) where complex economic transactions legally require elder 'trust-anchoring,' creating genuine demand for elder participation that funds itself.
how_it_works: Join an ICP (Intergenerational Cooperative Pool) recruited for income-pattern diversity. Open banking auto-sweeps fixed % of ICP aggregate income (not yours—the group's). Your variable income disappears into group smoothing. Transactions above thresholds require triadic signing: primary parties + registered elder trust-anchor whose 20+ years tenure provides legally-binding stability witness. Elders earn ongoing compensation for trust-anchoring, not pension drawdown. Your 'retirement' = ascending to trust-anchor status where your weave-position generates income. Research needed: legal frameworks for mandatory triadic signing, optimal ICP composition algorithms, trust-anchor liability structures.
what_is_new: First system where elders earn PRESENT compensation for ongoing participation rather than drawing down past contributions. First where retirement security is network position, not accumulated wealth. First where income smoothing happens at cooperative level before contribution, making individual variability literally invisible.
why_it_works: Variable earners can't save consistently but CAN maintain relationships consistently—even thin relationships persist through lean months. By measuring weave-tenure rather than deposit amounts, we ask for what they can actually provide. The wealthy need trust-anchors for sophisticated transactions, making elder-support self-funding rather than redistributive. Elders aren't charity cases—they're infrastructure.
why_it_fails: Legal requirements for triadic signing face fierce lobbying resistance—the wealthy prefer frictionless transactions. ICPs may stratify by class despite intentions. Trust-anchor liability creates risk elders may avoid. Those who can't/won't join ICPs receive no benefit. Some will game the system by maintaining hollow relationships. The requirement for 20+ year tenure excludes late joiners.
medium_term: Pilot ICPs demonstrate lower retirement anxiety and higher elder economic participation. 'Weave-tenure score' appears alongside credit metrics. Trust-anchor registries emerge. Variable earners report transformed relationship with retirement: from 'how much saved?' to 'how deeply woven?'
long_term_vision: Retirement reframed from wealth-accumulation to position-in-still-functioning-relationships. Variable earners—whose many gigs created many relationship-nodes—become the most securely woven. Elders aren't pensioners but active economic infrastructure whose trust-anchoring is genuinely necessary. The question shifts from 'how much have you saved?' to 'which dances do you know, and who will keep dancing with you?' Security comes not from what you hold but from being woven into patterns that persist.

label: Infrastructure-First Retirement Collective
design_principles: Build what surrounds you, not what you withdraw. The infrastructure IS retirement—not a proxy for purchasing it. Labor and presence substitute for money when money is scarce.
core_mechanism: Contributions build actual retirement infrastructure (housing, care systems, community spaces); access comes through membership, not account balance.
how_it_works: Join a regional Infrastructure Collective. High income: fund construction of co-housing, care facilities, caregiver training programs. Low income: contribute labor (construction, care, governance, maintenance). All labor types count toward membership-years. No individual balance—only collective infrastructure quality metrics. At retirement age: access the infrastructure you helped build. Quality of retirement = quality of what exists, shared equally among members. Research needed: membership-year equivalency formulas for different labor types, infrastructure quality certification, regional collective governance models.
what_is_new: First system where the retirement asset is literal infrastructure rather than financial claims. You can't 'run out'—the infrastructure either exists or doesn't. Variable earners contribute through labor when money is impossible. Question shifts from 'how much have I saved?' to 'how much have WE built?'
why_it_works: Dollars are proxies for purchasing infrastructure access, but infrastructure often doesn't exist at any price. By building the infrastructure directly, variable earners contribute through available labor, immune to income fluctuations. The asset is inherently communal—you can't hoard a community, and everyone benefits from quality improvements. This matches how retirement actually works: it's not the balance, it's what surrounds you.
why_it_fails: Massive coordination problem: infrastructure takes decades to build, early members bear costs while late members free-ride on completed facilities. Quality variance between regional collectives creates inequality. Political capture of governance. Members who contributed labor but move regions lose access. Those who can't contribute labor AND lack income are excluded. Infrastructure maintenance is perpetual—who pays after builders retire?
medium_term: Pilot collectives demonstrate that retirement quality correlates with infrastructure investment, not individual account size. 'Infrastructure-years' becomes a recognized retirement metric alongside savings. Labor-contribution exchanges standardize. Variable earners report transformed relationship with retirement: they can always contribute something.
long_term_vision: Retirement security measured by what surrounds you, not what you hold. Regional collectives compete on infrastructure quality—care facilities, co-housing, community spaces—not fund returns. Variable earners thrive because labor is always available even when money isn't. The anxious question 'am I saving enough?' dissolves into the collective question 'are we building enough?' Retirement becomes something you participate in creating rather than purchase access to.

label: Instance-Identity Covenant
design_principles: Count participation events, not amounts. Identity levels compound from instances. Constitutional covenant auto-activates at threshold. Funding and claiming are separate systems.
core_mechanism: Every income event = 1 cryptographic instance regardless of amount. Instances compound into permanent public identity levels. Constitutional guarantee auto-pays at Level 7 + age 62.
how_it_works: Payment processors/platforms attest 'income event occurred' (not amounts) to public chain. Each attestation = 1 instance. Every 100 instances = +1 identity level (permanent, public, like verified badge). Constitutional amendment guarantees sustenance to L7+ citizens at 62. No application—level IS qualification. Progressive income tax funds the Covenant Fund; instance-counting determines claims. Hardship attestations earn reduced intent-instances. Research needed: attestation standards across payment types, constitutional amendment strategy, identity level display protocols.
what_is_new: First system where a $5 gig and $5000 contract earn identical retirement credit (1 instance each). Funding is progressive; claiming is egalitarian. Variable earners with many small income events accumulate faster than steady earners. No balance to track—only permanent identity level that cannot decrease.
why_it_works: Variable earners fail amount-tracking systems but PARTICIPATE more frequently than steady earners. A gig worker with 50 monthly payments accumulates 50x faster than someone with 1 paycheck. By counting events not dollars, we reward what irregular earners actually do: constant economic participation. The public identity level eliminates bureaucracy—qualification is self-evident. Constitutional entrenchment makes the covenant as stable as voting rights.
why_it_fails: High earners may refuse to participate in a system where their $50k instance equals a $50 instance—perceived unfairness regardless of separate progressive funding. Attestation fraud through fake micro-transactions is possible (though detectable). Constitutional amendments are politically difficult. Public identity levels may create social stratification. Those with very few income events (subsistence farmers, long-term unemployed) may never reach threshold despite genuine need.
medium_term: Identity levels appear on government IDs and financial profiles. Gig platforms compete on instance-attestation speed. 'Instances this year' becomes a recognized metric. Variable earners report transformed relationship with retirement: every small payment advances their level. L7 becomes a celebrated life milestone.
long_term_vision: Retirement security measured by participation frequency rather than savings rate. Variable earners—whose fragmented income once disqualified them—become the fastest accumulators because every gig, every sale, every shift adds an instance. The anxious question 'have I saved enough?' dissolves into the simpler question 'what level am I?' The answer is always visible, always public, always permanent. Economic participation becomes its own reward: each transaction builds identity, and identity triggers rights.

label: Crisis Navigation Trust
design_principles: Survival knowledge is retirement capital. Low months build expertise; high months build credibility. Collective navigation multiplies extraction. Irregular income maintains capacities steady earners lose.
core_mechanism: Pooled survival-system navigation expertise + verified solidarity credibility, deployed as coordinated 'navigation swarms' that extract maximum resources from existing programs when elder members hit genuine crisis.
how_it_works: Join trust. Low-income months: navigate survival systems (food banks, housing programs, medical aid), document working pathways, verify peers' situations, maintain network ties—this IS contribution. High-income months: contribute money, maintain knowledge through monthly navigation check-ins with 3+ low-income members, demonstrate solidarity via verified underconsumption (open banking shows you chose X when you could afford Y). Elder crisis triggers navigation swarm: 20+ members with current expertise coordinate resource extraction across every applicable program. Multiple credible attestations unlock tier-2 benefits. Pool compensates swarm-hours. Research needed: knowledge documentation protocols, geographic coverage thresholds, solidarity verification standards.
what_is_new: First system where low-income months are productive expertise-building, not absence. First where irregular income creates better contributors (they maintain survival knowledge steady earners lose within months). First where collective navigation is multiplicative—coordinated expertise extracts resources individual navigation misses entirely.
why_it_works: Existing programs are massively underutilized—people don't know about them, can't navigate applications, lack credible attestation. Individual navigation captures maybe 25% of available resources; a coordinated swarm with current knowledge captures 80%+. The multiplication is real: different people know different systems, multiple attestations unlock additional tiers, coordinated timing creates institutional response. Irregular earners naturally maintain this expertise; it's their survival. Steady earners lose it within months of stability.
why_it_fails: Navigation expertise is geographically specific and rapidly changing—requires high membership density per region. Knowledge maintenance during high months feels burdensome. Tacit knowledge is hard to document. Systems may adapt to close pathways swarms exploit. Adverse selection: steady earners may join for benefits without contributing current expertise. Swarm coordination is administratively complex. Some will resent that their 'bad months' are now described as 'training.'
medium_term: 'Navigation currency' appears on financial profiles—how current is your survival expertise? Gig platforms integrate knowledge-contribution tracking. Irregular earners report transformed relationship with low months: earning expertise, not just surviving. Social services observe improved program utilization in Trust-dense regions.
long_term_vision: Retirement security rests on belonging to expertise-rich navigation networks, not accumulated savings. Variable earners—forced to constantly navigate survival systems—become essential infrastructure. Their knowledge is the only current knowledge; their attestations the only credible ones. The question shifts from 'how much saved?' to 'how current is your expertise, and who will deploy it for you?' Income irregularity transforms from retirement threat to competitive advantage. Those who struggled most know the most—and that knowledge, coordinated, extracts more than savings ever could.

label: Promise Maturation Fund
design_principles: Commitment itself is the compounding asset. Promises made young cure into security. Early issuance beats late capital. Variable income isthe native case, not exception.
core_mechanism: Blockchain-registered Promise Tokens that appreciate 3% annually ('cure') regardless of fulfillment, with completion bonuses when eventually fulfilled via cash or labor.
how_it_works: Register free, get Commitment Account. Issue Promise Tokens anytime: 'I will contribute $X or Y labor-hours.' Tokens timestamped, appreciation tracked. During income: auto-fulfill oldest tokens via open banking—receive (base value × years curing × 1.5 completion bonus). During drought: issue new tokens (costs nothing). Unfulfilled tokens at retirement pay 40% of cured value. Fund invests fulfilled contributions; AI improves fulfillment prediction over generations. Research needed: optimal curing rates, labor-valuation standards, anti-gaming detection.
what_is_new: First system where COMMITMENT compounds, not cash. You can contribute the promise itself when you have nothing. Early-issued, late-fulfilled tokens beat late contributions mathematically. Variable earners—with more droughts—have more promise-issuance opportunities.
why_it_works: Variable earners can't reliably produce cash but CAN reliably produce commitment. Every drought is a promise-making opportunity. A token issued at 25, curing for 20 years, fulfilled at 45, curing 20 more years = exponentially more retirement value than cash deposited at 55. The promise itself is productive because it creates accountability, improves fund prediction, and compounds trust.
why_it_fails: Gaming via promises never intended for fulfillment (the 40% unfulfilled value). Fund may be overwhelmed by labor promises it can't absorb. Fulfillment verification is administratively heavy. Those who die early after issuing many promises provide windfall to collective. Psychological burden of unfulfilled promises may deter participation.
medium_term: 'Promise age' appears on financial dashboards alongside savings rate. Gig platforms integrate automatic token issuance during detected income gaps. Variable earners report transformed relationship with droughts: each one is promise-making opportunity. Token fulfillment becomes celebrated milestone.
long_term_vision: Retirement security measured by commitment history, not contribution amounts. Variable earners—whose droughts forced more promise-issuance—retire most securely because they made the most early commitments. The anxious question 'am I saving enough?' becomes 'how long have my promises been curing?' Cash-poor youth systematically out-retire cash-rich middle age. Promise-keeping becomes the fundamental retirement skill, and every kept promise—no matter how small—compounds into security.

label: Baseline Gradient Access Collective
design_principles: Position, not behavior. Access, not balance. Collective stability through aggregate smoothing. Governance by net-receivers. Opt-out default shifts friction to non-participation.
core_mechanism: Real-time baseline-differential auto-sweep: open banking computes your income vs. collective rolling average; excess auto-transfers to access portfolio networks; deficit triggers micro-support flows. No conscious contribution—only position.
how_it_works: Join collective (default through employment contracts, opt-out available). Open banking links compute your position vs. collective baseline continuously. Above baseline: excess flows to purchase shares in housing cooperatives, healthcare mutuals, food security networks, social engagement pools. Below baseline: micro-support flows from collective buffer. Retirement: activate access across your portfolio networks—housing, health, food, social connection—rather than drawing down balance. Institutional anchor (credit union or government backstop) guarantees floor. Net-receivers govern; their weighted votes determine collective policy. Research needed: optimal baseline calculation windows, access network interoperability standards, opt-out friction calibration.
what_is_new: First system where contribution is determined by real-time position relative to moving baseline—not amounts, patterns, frequency, tenure, or any individual characteristic. First where retirement is access to multi-dimensional cooperative networks rather than monetary balance. Net-receivers govern, inverting typical power dynamics.
why_it_works: Variable earners fail systems requiring consistent action because they can't reliably perform any action. This system requires no action—only existence at some income level. The baseline comparison happens automatically; the portfolio acquisition happens automatically; even governance happens by default (receivers vote by being receivers). Individual income chaos is irrelevant because N diverse earners' chaos smooths to predictable aggregate flow. The access portfolio model eliminates 'running out'—networks either exist or don't.
why_it_fails: Open banking dependency creates surveillance concerns and excludes cash-economy workers. Access network quality varies by region—rural collectives may have thin portfolios. Institutional anchor may be captured or underfunded. Opt-out default faces political resistance from those ideologically opposed to collectivization. Net-receiver governance may make poor decisions due to information asymmetry about collective finances. The 'you own nothing, you access everything' model may feel precarious even if functionally superior.
medium_term: Employment contracts default to collective membership; opting out requires explicit action. 'Access portfolio diversity score' appears on financial profiles. Housing/health/food cooperatives federate into interoperable networks. Net-receiver governance councils emerge as influential policy voices. Variable earners report transformed relationship with income—fluctuation becomes irrelevant to security.
long_term_vision: Retirement security decoupled from individual saving behavior entirely. Variable earners—once most vulnerable—become indistinguishable from steady earners because the system never measured individual consistency. The anxious question 'am I saving enough?' dissolves into 'am I in a collective?'—and the answer is usually yes, by default. Access replaces accumulation as the retirement paradigm; networks replace balances. Those who fluctuate most contribute exactly as much security as those who earn steadily, because only aggregate flow matters. Income chaos stops being personal failure and becomes—at collective scale—statistical noise.

label: Depth-Guarantee Stability Fund
design_principles: Guarantee depth > contribution consistency. Stabilize the worker now; retirement emerges from the stability infrastructure. Nested institutions multiply security.
core_mechanism: Contributions flow through nested guarantee layers (local→regional→national→reinsurance). Each layer guarantees 80% of layer below. 4-layer depth = 99.84% effective guarantee, enabling stability services NOW backed by near-risk-free assets.
how_it_works: Join local collective (~100 members, trade/neighborhood). Quarterly 'tidal windows' accept any contribution. Funds immediately flow through 4 guarantee layers. Because assets are deeply guaranteed, fund safely provides stability services during accumulation: emergency credit lines, income smoothing, institutional verification for gig platforms. Stability services funded by 15% of returns; 85% accumulates. Retirement = guaranteed floor (from stacked guarantees) + variable portion. Irregular contributions gain full guarantee depth immediately. Research needed: guarantee layer independence requirements, stability service actuarial models, cross-layer correlation limits.
what_is_new: First where GUARANTEE DEPTH (number of independent institutional guarantees) determines value, not contribution patterns. First where stability services DURING accumulation are the primary benefit, enabled by deeply guaranteed assets, with retirement as infrastructure byproduct.
why_it_works: Irregular earners can't sacrifice current stability for future security—every drought forces impossible choices. By providing stability NOW (backed by assets so deeply guaranteed they're effectively risk-free), the system eliminates the trade-off. You receive stability services; the asset base generating them becomes your retirement. The guarantee depth means small irregular contributions are immediately as secure as any pension.
why_it_fails: Requires massive institutional scale—meaningful 4-layer depth needs regional/national coordination. Correlated failures (economic collapse) could pierce all layers simultaneously. Stability service costs could cannibalize retirement accumulation if not actuarially managed. Early members bear institution-building costs while late members free-ride on established guarantee depth.
medium_term: Gig platforms integrate as first-layer collectives. 'Guarantee depth' appears as retirement metric. Variable earners report transformed relationship with income—fluctuations no longer threaten retirement OR current stability. Stability service usage data enables predictive support.
long_term_vision: Retirement security measured by guarantee depth, not savings rate. The anxious question 'am I saving enough?' dissolves into 'how deeply guaranteed am I?' Variable earners achieve identical security to steady earners because guarantee depth applies immediately regardless of contribution pattern. The retirement system becomes invisible infrastructure—you simply have stability services throughout life, and when you stop working, the asset base that funded them becomes your income. Saving and stability are the same thing.
